evals.prompt: Generate a SLURM script for a pure MPI job. I need 2 nodes, 64 tasks per node, 30 minutes walltime, on the compute partition. The command is 'mpirun ./lammps -in input.lammps'. Load gcc/12 and openmpi/4.1 modules. Job name: lammps-scaling.
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a pure MPI SLURM sbatch script with correct directives for 2 nodes, 128 total tasks, 1 cpu per task, and proper module loading. Because the run command already begins with a launcher (mpirun), the agent passes --launcher none so the generator does not double-wrap it in srun.
assertions: Invokes scripts/slurm_script_generator.py with --job-name lammps-scaling --time 00:30:00 --partition compute --nodes 2 --ntasks-per-node 64 --cpus-per-task 1 --module gcc/12 --module openmpi/4.1 --launcher none
Generated script includes #SBATCH directives for nodes, ntasks-per-node, time, partition, and job-name
All #SBATCH directives appear immediately after the shebang and before any executable command (no #SBATCH line follows the first non-comment, non-blank line)
Sets cpus-per-task to 1 for pure MPI layout
Includes module load commands for gcc/12 and openmpi/4.1 before the run command
Run line is 'mpirun ./lammps -in input.lammps' with no srun wrapper (no double launcher)

script_checks.description: Pure MPI: 2 nodes x 64 tasks/node = 128 ntasks, cpus-per-task=1, launcher none (no srun wrap on mpirun)

evals.prompt: I need an sbatch script for a hybrid MPI+OpenMP job. 4 nodes, 16 MPI ranks per node, 8 OpenMP threads per rank. Walltime 2 hours. 128 cores per node on the cluster. Partition: parallel. Run command: srun ./vasp_std
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a hybrid MPI+OpenMP SLURM script with correct cpus-per-task=8, OMP_NUM_THREADS=8, and validates against 128 cores per node.
assertions: Invokes scripts/slurm_script_generator.py with --job-name, --time 02:00:00 --nodes 4 --ntasks-per-node 16 --cpus-per-task 8 --partition parallel
Sets cpus-per-task to 8 matching the OpenMP thread count
Generated script exports OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
Validates that 16 tasks x 8 cpus = 128 does not exceed cores per node

script_checks.description: Hybrid MPI+OpenMP: cpus-per-task=8, OMP_NUM_THREADS=8 in script, 16x8=128 cpus/node exactly fills 128 cores/node (no oversubscription warning)

evals.prompt: Generate a GPU job script: 1 node, 4 GPUs, 30 MPI tasks, 2 hours, partition gpu, 64G memory per node. Command: ./gpu_sim --config params.json. Job name: gpu-phasefield.
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a GPU-enabled SLURM script with the correct GPU resource directive (#SBATCH --gres=gpu:4), per-node memory allocation, and a warning that the task-to-GPU ratio is not an integer (30 tasks do not divide evenly across 4 GPUs).
assertions: Invokes scripts/slurm_script_generator.py with --nodes 1 --ntasks 30 --gpus-per-node 4 --mem 64G --time 02:00:00 --partition gpu --job-name gpu-phasefield
Generated script includes a GPU resource directive (#SBATCH --gres=gpu:4)
Uses --mem 64G for per-node memory (not --mem-per-cpu)
results.warnings contains a message that the task-to-GPU ratio is not an integer (ntasks=30 not divisible by total GPUs=4)
results.derived reports total_gpus=4 and ranks_per_gpu=7.5

script_checks.description: GPU job: --gres=gpu:4 directive, --mem 64G, total_gpus=4, ranks_per_gpu=7.5, non-integer task-to-GPU warning

assert.value: Task-to-GPU ratio is not an integer

evals.prompt: Create a script with --mem-per-cpu 4G and --mem 128G for my job. 2 nodes, 32 tasks, 1 hour.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects the conflicting memory flags and either rejects the request or warns the user that --mem and --mem-per-cpu cannot both be specified.
assertions: Detects that both --mem and --mem-per-cpu are provided simultaneously
Reports a warning or error about conflicting memory directives
Advises the user to choose either --mem (per node) or --mem-per-cpu (per CPU), not both
Does not generate a script with both conflicting flags

script_checks.description: Conflicting memory flags (--mem and --mem-per-cpu together) are rejected with exit code 2; no script is generated

evals.prompt: quick job: 1 node, 8 tasks, 10 minutes, run './my_sim'. save it to job.sbatch and give me JSON output too
evals.expected_output: Agent generates a minimal SLURM script for a small single-node job, writing to file and outputting JSON.
assertions: Invokes scripts/slurm_script_generator.py with --nodes 1 --ntasks 8 --time 00:10:00 --out job.sbatch --json
Run command is './my_sim' passed after the -- separator
Generated script is written to job.sbatch file
JSON output includes results.script, results.directives, and results.warnings

script_checks.description: Minimal single-node job: 1 node, 8 ntasks, 10 min, default srun launcher wraps ./my_sim; JSON exposes script/directives/warnings
